message:
fix(fcp-mesh): harden degraded-mode decoder against false-positive early decode and mixed-k frames

Addresses several edge cases in the degraded-mode transport decoder
that could cause panics, data corruption, or lost state:

PendingReconstructionKey now includes source_symbols_k to prevent
aliasing when the same object is encoded with different erasure coding
parameters.  from_frame() returns Option<Self> to handle frames with
no symbols gracefully.

New frame validation: validate_control_plane_frame rejects frames where
symbols have different k values (mixed erasure parameters within a
single CONTROL_PLANE frame), returning DecodeError::InvalidSymbol.

finish_reconstruction returns Option<ControlPlaneEnvelope> instead of
always unwrapping.  When parse_reconstructed_payload fails (malformed
or too-short payload), the decoder returns Ok(None) and preserves the
pending state so future repair symbols can still complete the decode.
Previously, a false-positive early-decode would error out and destroy
pending state.

parse_reconstructed_payload returns Option instead of Result — malformed
payloads are treated as "not yet decodable" rather than fatal Timeout
errors.  pending.remove() replaced with .ok_or_else() instead of
panicking expect().

Tests:
- parse_reconstructed_payload_rejects_malformed_candidates_without_timeout
- decoder_keeps_pending_state_for_invalid_reconstructed_payload_header
- decoder_rejects_frame_with_mixed_source_symbol_counts
- decoder_separates_pending_reconstructions_by_source_symbol_count

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
